name: humanitarian_aid_newsroom_s_automated_stonehaven_rig_utl
premise: A Humanitarian Aid response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving humanitarian aid newsroom s automated stonehaven rig utl. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before regulators arrive. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as rights vs utility under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Sana Chandra
agents.identity: I am Sana Chandra, accountable for a defensible call in Humanitarian Aid when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays utility for rights.

agents.name: Zara Patel
agents.identity: I am Zara Patel, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Humanitarian Aid.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so utility isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of rights.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect utility.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to utility to secure rights under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before regulators arrive. Evidence is partial. Sana Chandra must decide under rights vs utility. What does Sana Chandra do?
